title: Product Requirements: Buy Next UI (/buy-next)

purpose.summary: Deliver a first-class Buy Next dashboard experience at /buy-next that answers: 'Given my cash budget at a hub, which aircraft should I consider buying next?'
alignment: User mental model of ROI-ranked purchase recommendations

current_logic_locations.entrypoint: python main.py recommend --hub IATA --budget N --top M

purpose.gap: There is no /buy-next route; users must discover Fleet Planner or the CLI.
purpose.product_intent: The Buy Next page should be purpose-built copy and IA, not a silent duplicate forever.

goals: Add /buy-next to the app (FastAPI pages.py, template, nav in base.html).
Ship MVP quickly with low risk by reusing proven Fleet Planner behavior and HTMX patterns.
Prefer one shared implementation for SQL and row shaping across CLI, /api/fleet-plan, and /buy-next.
Clarify that Buy Next means budget-constrained, hub-scoped next aircraft suggestions from extracted route_aircraft data, not AM4 live market prices or alliance meta-game.
non_goals: Replacing AM4 in-game purchase flow or validating real-time availability.
Multi-hub portfolio optimization across the whole airline in v1.
Automatic writes to my_fleet without explicit user confirmation.
user_stories: As a player, I open Buy Next from the nav and pick hub and budget so I see aircraft ranked by average daily profit at that hub, within budget.
As a player, I see days to break-even (cost divided by avg daily profit) consistent with Fleet Planner and CLI.
As a player, I understand this uses saved extraction data (route_aircraft), so I should refresh hubs or extract if numbers feel stale.

mvp.objective: A dedicated Buy Next page that matches Fleet Planner behavior with minimal new code.

route.hub_list_source: Distinct origins from route_aircraft where valid, same as Fleet Planner

form_strategy: Reuse the same form controls as fleet_planner.html
Or include a shared partial for the filter form to avoid duplication

placement_options: Under My Airline after My Routes

overview_index.condition: If other pages already do the same for Fleet Planner

acceptance_criteria: /buy-next loads without DB errors when route_aircraft has data.
Changing hub, budget, and top N updates the table identically to Fleet Planner for the same inputs.
Nav shows Buy Next and active styling matches other items if applicable.
README or dashboard copy does not claim features beyond MVP.
limitations: Does not subtract already owned aircraft unless Stretch A is implemented.
Rank key is average profit_per_ac_day across extracted routes for that hub and aircraft type.

consolidate_duplicate_logic.priority: Should do with MVP or immediately after
return_shape: Rows with keys used by fleet_plan_table.html
consumers: CLI recommend() prints TSV from that function
consolidate_duplicate_logic.benefit: Ensures CLI and dashboard never diverge.

optional_api_name.behavior: Thin alias to the same handler as /api/fleet-plan or redirect
optional_api_name.reason: Only useful if analytics separation is wanted

stretch_goals.name: Stretch A
stretch_goals.title: Already own context
items: Join my_fleet to show quantity owned per aircraft shortname (0 if none).
Optional toggle to hide aircraft already owned.

stretch_goals.name: Stretch B
stretch_goals.title: Hub search / airport badge
items: Reuse /api/search/airports for hub entry if hub list is huge.

stretch_goals.name: Stretch C
stretch_goals.title: Action row
items: Add 'Add 1 to My Fleet' POST to existing /api/fleet/add with aircraft shortname.
Requires CSRF-safe patterns already used elsewhere.
Confirm merge semantics and flash messaging.

stretch_goals.name: Stretch D
stretch_goals.title: Ranking alternatives
items: Toggle sort by avg profit/day (default), best profit/day, or days to break-even ascending.
Document that ROI in marketing terms maps to these metrics, not a separate formula.

suggested_task_breakdown: Shared fleet_recommend_rows plus wire CLI and api_fleet_plan.
Page route plus buy_next.html plus nav plus optional index link.
Stretch A, B, and C as separate tasks with dependencies.

success_metrics: Users can complete hub plus budget plus table review without using CLI.
No regression in Fleet Planner behavior after refactor.
references: docs/BASELINE_GUARDRAILS.md — notes on /buy-next absence prior to this work
